home.hero.label: Embedded transformation / software delivery
home.hero.lead: I work embedded with the people building the software and stay close to the people accountable for the outcome. My focus is the gap where leadership intent, team reality, process, and AI adoption have to become one workable delivery system.

work.img.alt: Profile picture of Martin Trenker, independent software consultant
work.title: Martin Trenker
work.subtitle: Independent software consultant for embedded delivery, architecture, and AI with judgment
work.about.heading: About me
work.about.p1: Across 20 engagements, the useful work was rarely only technical or only organizational. Delivery improved when code, ownership, feedback, and trust were handled together.
work.about.p2: I work close to teams that build the product, help make decisions explicit, and support sponsors who want faster delivery without more theater.
work.contact.heading: Contact
work.skills.heading: Skills
work.skills.programming.heading: Programming
work.skills.org.heading: Organizational
work.section.cleanAgile.heading: Architecture and safer change
work.section.cleanAgile.p: Boundaries, state, tests, and architecture that let teams change software without creating a new knot every sprint.
work.section.learner.heading: Alignment, incentives, and ownership
work.section.learner.p: Clearer reviews, smaller slices, visible trade-offs, and enough trust between sponsors, middle layers, and teams for change to survive daily pressure.
work.section.automation.heading: AI adoption with judgment
work.section.automation.p: Assistants, agents, and automation where the work is clear enough to describe, review, and own. If the process is vague, I fix that before adding AI.
work.projects.heading: Projects
work.spotlight.heading: Selected projects
work.spotlight.lead: A few engagements with enough detail to show the work itself: embedded delivery, architecture, mentoring, and practical change inside existing organizations.

work.timeline.heading: Project timeline
work.cta.heading: If this sounds close to what your team needs, let’s talk.
work.cta.lead: Send a short note about the team, the transformation pressure, and what breaks between intent and execution. If I can help, I will. If not, I will say so honestly.
contact.heading: Tell me what you want to improve.
contact.lead: A short note about the team, the delivery situation, and what should get better is enough. I will reply directly.
contact.context.heading: What happens next
contact.context.response: I read every message myself and usually reply within one business day.
contact.context.process: The first useful step is normally a short call to understand the situation and decide whether I can help.
contact.context.selectivity: I work with a small number of clients at any time because embedded work needs attention and trust. If I can help, I will say what that could look like. If I cannot, I will say so honestly.

privacy.reviewFlow.heading: Review flow (LinkedIn sign-in and feedback form)
privacy.reviewFlow.p1: When you follow a personal review invitation link to clean.dev, you may optionally sign in with LinkedIn via OAuth 2.0. LinkedIn will share your display name, email address, and a unique LinkedIn profile identifier with this application. LinkedIn session data is not written to the application database.
privacy.reviewFlow.p2: Whether or not you use LinkedIn, you fill in a form with your name, email address, an optional role, your relationship to the project, and your written feedback. Explicit consent is required before the form can be submitted.
privacy.reviewFlow.p3: On submission, all form data and any LinkedIn identity are forwarded immediately by email to the controller. Nothing is written to the application database.
privacy.reviewFlow.recipientValue: Email inbox of the controller. The email delivery provider {provider} acts as a processor according to Art. 28 GDPR.
privacy.reviewFlow.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. a GDPR (your explicit consent to testimonial use of your name and feedback); Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in confirming reviewer identity via LinkedIn).
privacy.reviewFlow.retentionValue: Submission data is forwarded immediately and is not retained by the application. The controller's email inbox retains the received message subject to standard email retention practices.
privacy.reviewFlow.linkedinNote: LinkedIn identity verification is handled by LinkedIn Ireland Unlimited Company, Wilton Plaza, Wilton Place, Dublin 2, Ireland. LinkedIn processes your data according to their own privacy policy.
home.hero.thesis.label: thesis /
home.hero.thesis.body: Transformation usually fails in the middle: goals get softened, incentives pull apart, and teams receive contradictions too late. AI adoption has the same problem. The durable value will come from clear work design, trusted feedback loops, useful assistants, and deterministic tooling that teams can actually own.
home.profileCard.meta3: close to the people building the product
home.profileCard.note: I work close enough to see where delivery actually gets stuck, then help remove the causes.
home.availability.label: Current capacity
home.availability.now.label: now
home.availability.next.label: first step
home.position.heading: Position
home.position.meta: what this is / what it is not
home.position.is.label: this is
home.position.is.1: working embedded in teams that build the product, not advising from the outside
home.position.is.2: connecting leadership intent with code, reviews, planning, incentives, and trade-offs
home.position.is.3: treating agile, process, and AI as connected transformation work
home.position.is.4: direct, honest consulting with respect for leaders, middle layers, and the people doing the work
home.position.not.label: this is not
home.position.not.2: cargo cult agile or AI adoption
home.operating.heading: Operating model
home.operating.embed.title: Keep intent connected
home.operating.embed.body: I work in the team while keeping the leadership intent visible. The job is to stop good goals from being watered down before they reach planning, code, review, and release habits.
home.operating.embed.measure: Signals: goal clarity, decision latency, trust, WIP, handoffs
home.operating.system.title: Align the operating system
home.operating.system.body: Process change only sticks when incentives, ownership, feedback loops, and architecture point in the same direction. I help make the real system visible enough to change.
home.operating.system.measure: Signals: ownership gaps, escalation paths, review depth, rollback rate
home.operating.ai.title: Make AI adoption usable
home.operating.ai.body: AI is useful when it becomes part of the work system: clear use cases, local or private assistants where they make sense, deterministic tooling around them, and human review where judgment matters.
home.operating.ai.measure: Signals: context quality, review load, cost, risk, repeatability
home.workbench.label: From the workbench
home.workbench.one.text: why agile and AI transformations run into similar tensions
home.workbench.two.text: how trust and sponsorship change team-level adoption
home.workbench.three.text: what premature standardization breaks before it helps
home.workbench.four.text: clean code as the base layer for useful AI
home.topics.heading: Topics
home.topics.cleanCode.title: Clean code and safer change
home.topics.cleanCode.body: Boundaries, naming, tests, refactoring, and code that stays understandable under pressure.
home.topics.agile.title: Practical agile inside teams
home.topics.agile.body: Learning speed, trust, alignment, slicing, WIP, and the habits that make adaptation possible without relying on ceremonies alone.
home.topics.aiDelivery.title: Delivery with AI in the loop
home.topics.aiDelivery.body: AI adoption as embedded transformation work: leadership intent, team habits, clear work packages, local assistants, deterministic tooling, review points, and ownership that grows from practice.
home.topics.leadership.title: Technical leadership and decisions
home.topics.leadership.body: ADRs, ownership, disagreement, escalation, and keeping technical judgment visible.
home.engagements.heading: Engagement log
home.contact.heading: Let's talk
home.contact.lead: If strategy is clear in meetings but weak in the actual delivery system, we can start with where the thread breaks and see whether I can help reconnect it.
home.fit.buyer.label: who this helps
home.fit.buyer.body: Engineering leaders, transformation sponsors, tech leads, product people, and teams who need strategy to survive the path from steering meetings to actual delivery.
home.fit.shape.label: where it helps
home.fit.shape.body: A team or product area where goals, incentives, process, technical reality, or AI adoption pressure no longer pull in the same direction.
home.fit.mode.label: how I work
home.fit.mode.body: Embedded in teams, close to the people building the product, and connected enough to leadership to make change possible beyond local heroics.
home.fit.not.label: less useful for
home.fit.not.body: AI programs built around hype, copied rollout playbooks, or transformations that mainly need compliance instead of changed work.
home.contact.note: A short note about the team, the transformation pressure, and where execution currently breaks is enough.
work.hero.label: Selected work / real project data
work.hero.heading: Project history from inside real teams.
work.hero.lead: A portfolio of real engagements across retail, finance, media, travel, automotive, consulting, and industrial software. The pattern is consistent: transformation works when leadership intent, team practice, technical quality, and trust improve together.

work.availability.text: Current engagement wrapping up in July 2026 after 2.5 years — taking on new work from September 2026.

work.lab.meta: own production systems, self-funded and self-operated
work.lab.lead: Proof that does not come from client work: production systems I build, run, and pay for myself. Experiments stay out — everything here is in real use.

blog.note: Every post is written by me. I use AI for research, critique, and editing pressure, not to outsource the point of view.
blog.empty: No posts yet. The first essays are in progress.
